Off Calendar Jul-31-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Donna O'Balle

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings brought on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it because it owed no duty to plaintiff is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff did not substantively oppose defendant's motion. Plaintiff's request for leave to amend is denied because plaintiff did not demonstrate a reasonable possibility that the defect - lack of duty - can be cured by the proposed twenty-second cause of action. (See Campbell v. Ford Motor Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15; Kesner v. Superior Court (May 15, 2014) 226 cal.App.4th 251; Haver v. BNSF Railway Co. (June 3, 2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1104.) Counsel for defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
